---
title: "MIForce"
generated: true
description: "Closed-form Mattes mutual-information force (the cross-modal fast path)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.register`](../nitrix-register.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.register._force`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MIForce -->
```python
class MIForce
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:MIForce -->
Closed-form Mattes mutual-information force (the cross-modal fast path).

The Tier-1 analogue of [`MetricForce`](../nitrix-register/MetricForce.qmd) wrapping a mutual-information
metric: drives a dense-field recipe by $\text{mi\_grad} \cdot
\nabla\,\text{warped}$ (the closed-form `metrics.mi_grad`, i.e.
$\partial\,\mathrm{MI} / \partial\,\text{warped}$) instead of a full
`jax.grad` of the soft-histogram cost every iteration -- the fast
cross-modal deformable path, with the histogram-scatter autodiff tape
removed.

Like [`MetricForce`](../nitrix-register/MetricForce.qmd) for a histogram metric (and for the same reason --
mutual information is not a spatial mean, so its raw $1/N$ gradient is
an arbitrary, metric-scale-dependent magnitude), the force is normalised to
a controlled per-voxel RMS `magnitude`: this is what makes it a true
*drop-in* fast replacement (the same controlled step the clamped SyN and
unclamped Demons drivers need), and it agrees with the autodiff
[`MetricForce`](../nitrix-register/MetricForce.qmd) in **direction** to the empty-bin tolerance and in
**magnitude** by construction (the parity oracle).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MIForce.bind -->
```python
bind(fixed: Float[Array, '*spatial'], *, ndim: int, rel_spacing: RelSpacing = None) -> _BoundMI
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Bind to `fixed`, carrying the histogram and sampling settings.

The joint histogram depends on both images at the current warp, so no
image-dependent state is hoisted here; the binding simply captures the
fixed image alongside the configured binning.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:MIForce.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(bins: int = 32, range_moving: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, range_fixed: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, magnitude: float = 0.5, sample_stride: int = 1, normalized: bool = False) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:MIForce -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
